source.releaseCommitMessage: Corregir señales SEO de Search Console

releaseContents: Canonical, Open Graph, Twitter and WebSite JSON-LD URLs aligned with the production www host.
Privacy page added to sitemap.xml and crawler directives simplified to avoid blocking public resources.
Root-level development artifacts changed from public static responses to 404 responses.
One semantic level-one heading added to the application shell without visual layout changes.
Deterministic server and UI regression coverage for all changed SEO signals.
excludedLocalChanges: the connected-user ARIA test hunks in tests/run.mjs

preDeployChecks.formatCheck: not clean: pre-existing repository-wide Prettier debt remains; no bulk formatting was applied

postDeployVerification.shell: 200 from the www domain and Render origin
postDeployVerification.authentication: Google, Turnstile and email-code preflight checks all configured and safe
postDeployVerification.bootstrap: 200 guest payload, Secure HttpOnly cookie, no session ID in response body

seo.semanticH1: present in the live browser accessibility tree
seo.robotsTxt: 200, blocks /api/ and references the www sitemap
seo.unpublishedOriginRoutes: all six return 404 from Render origin

postDeployVerification.browser: mobile Chromium check passed with expected title, h1, canonical, robots and footer links

metrics.assessment: No stop condition observed. CPU remained low and Render emitted no error-level logs.

edgeCache.status: one stale object remains temporarily cached
edgeCache.assessment: The deployed origin is correct. The old Cloudflare object will disappear when its existing edge TTL expires; no Cloudflare purge credential was available.

rollback.databaseCompatibility: No schema or data migration was included.
rollback.dataRecovery: Use the logical SQLite backup before considering a Render disk snapshot restore.

unresolvedRisks: Google Search Console can keep historical canonical and exclusion states until Google recrawls the affected URLs and sitemap.
Cloudflare temporarily retains one previously cached internal PNG until its four-hour edge TTL expires.
GitHub Actions reports a non-blocking warning that checkout@v4 and setup-node@v4 target the deprecated Node.js 20 action runtime.
The unrelated connected-user ARIA changes remain local and were intentionally not included in this production release.
